project.name: evoting
project.context: Web e-voting application using El Gamal homomorphic encryption as the security model.

designPhilosophy: Controlled, inspectable, and voter-first. The interface should make encrypted voting understandable without overclaiming production security.

visualDirection.anchor: tamper-evident ballot envelope under a counting-room lamp
distinctiveMoves: sealed receipt panel for encrypted ballot state
copper cryptographic accents separated from green verification state

usedSignals: official Tailwind CSS v4 setup guidance

conceptualAnchor.name: Counting-room ballot seal
conceptualAnchor.anchorReference: tamper-evident ballot envelope under a counting-room lamp
conceptualAnchor.sourceDomain: civic counting room and physical ballot custody
conceptualAnchor.specificReferencePoint: sealed ballot envelope, ledger ink, lamp-lit count table
conceptualAnchor.rationale: Voting needs trust, custody, and count clarity more than generic dashboard energy.
conceptualAnchor.signatureMotion: selection compresses into sealed receipt; tally bars rebalance after submission
conceptualAnchor.typographicDecision: display for election identity, plain text for voter instruction, tabular numerals for counts
conceptualAnchor.motionRiskBudget: short state transitions with reduced-motion fallback

derivedTokenLogic.anchorReference: tamper-evident ballot envelope under a counting-room lamp
derivedTokenLogic.colorDerivationSource: paper, ink, copper seal, verification mark, and caution states
derivedTokenLogic.spacingDerivationSource: ballot surfaces use compact inspection spacing with clear action zones
derivedTokenLogic.typographyDerivationSource: ledger counts use tabular numerals; instructions use plain readable text
derivedTokenLogic.motionDerivationSource: seal and tally transitions are short, deliberate, and non-decorative
derivedTokenLogic.validationRule: Every token must map to custody, voting, verification, or result reading.

motionPaletteDecision.paletteDecision: off-white ballot, ink, copper cryptographic accent, green verification, red blocking risk
motionPaletteDecision.motionDecision: medium-low density with explicit seal, submit, and tally update states
motionPaletteDecision.spatialDecision: 2D responsive app; no 3D because the workflow needs clarity and fast form completion

libraryDecisions.name: Next.js
libraryDecisions.purpose: App Router, route handlers, single deployable

mathSystems.crypto: El Gamal BigInt demo encryption and homomorphic multiplication

crossViewportAdaptation.desktop: voting, receipt, and results visible together

motionSystem.reducedMotion: remove transform motion and keep text/status feedback

componentMorphology.surfaces: ballot sheets and sealed receipt panels

reviewRubric: Does the UI make ballot custody clear?
Can a voter complete the main action on mobile?
Are proof and security claims honest?
Does the chart remain readable across widths?

implementation.stack: Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS v4, local shadcn/ui components, Recharts
